observations.statement: The staged contract documents POST /api/v2/disaster/spending_by_geography/ with required body keys filter, geo_layer, and spending_type, plus optional geo_layer_filters and scope.
The filter object documentation explicitly includes required filter.def_codes and optional filter.award_type_codes.
The documented response echoes geo_layer, scope, and spending_type and returns results rows with shape_code, amount, display_name, population, per_capita, and award_count.
The staged markdown contains an internal mismatch: the Schema block labels the POST body as type string while the attributes and example body clearly describe a JSON object payload.

source.title: Current raw MCP profile for disaster spending_by_geography

observations.statement: The current raw profile treats the request body as an object and preserves nested filter fields filter.time_period, filter.recipient_scope, and filter.recipient_locations in addition to documented filter.def_codes and filter.award_type_codes.
The current raw profile says scope defaults to recipient_location when omitted and notes geo_layer_filters act as an allowlist of shape codes for the chosen geography layer.

The current raw profile reports that results may include a null shape_code/display_name aggregate bucket when uncoded spending cannot be assigned to a geography, especially when geo_layer_filters are omitted.
The current raw profile includes both 400-style enum validation and 422-style minimum-items validation in examples, so callers should expect multiple validation status families.

source.title: Derived MCP coverage check for disaster spending_by_geography

observations.statement: The current endpoint-facing MCP surface exposes the top-level body keys filter, geo_layer, geo_layer_filters, scope, and spending_type, but coding agents still need nested filter paths such as filter.def_codes and shared search keys to build useful requests.
Because the endpoint is an aggregate geography surface with no paging or award identifiers, downstream drill-down usually requires a second endpoint keyed by the same DEFC and geography filters.

source.title: Live USAspending probes for disaster spending_by_geography on 2026-05-10

observations.statement: A minimal DEFC L state obligation request returned HTTP 200, confirming the route is live and that omitting scope defaults the response to recipient_location.
The live response included a row with null shape_code and null display_name, confirming an uncoded aggregate bucket that contradicts the documentation's required-string description for those fields.
The baseline state response included both positive and negative amounts; New Jersey appeared with a negative obligation total.

source.title: Live USAspending probes for disaster spending_by_geography on 2026-05-10

observations.statement: A filtered state outlay request with scope=place_of_performance returned HTTP 200, confirming that place_of_performance is accepted and echoed in the response.
geo_layer_filters=[CA,TX] limited the response to those two states and the response contained no null aggregate bucket in that filtered sample.

source.title: Live USAspending probes for disaster spending_by_geography on 2026-05-10

bodySample.detail: Field 'filter|def_codes' value '[]' is below min '1' items

observations.statement: Submitting an empty filter.def_codes array returned HTTP 422 with a minimum-items validation message.

source.title: Live USAspending probes for disaster spending_by_geography on 2026-05-10

observations.statement: Submitting lowercase def_codes returned HTTP 400 with the valid uppercase enum list, confirming DEFC codes are case-sensitive.

source.title: USAspending award search and shared search filter docs for state drilldown mapping

observations.statement: The spending_by_award search docs support def_codes alongside recipient_scope plus recipient_locations and place_of_performance_scope plus place_of_performance_locations, so disaster DEFC filters can be preserved while drilling from geography aggregates into award-level search.

The shared search-filter docs show state-level location objects formatted like {"country":"USA","state":"VA"}, which makes non-null disaster geography state shape_code values directly reusable as downstream award-search state filters.
